comparison_summary: The Ninja AF101 outperforms the Gourmia GAF400 with its 1550W power and superior construction, while the Gourmia counters with a bigger 5-quart basket at a much friendlier price. Most people will be happier with the Ninja's speed and longevity, but families on a budget get more capacity for less money with the Gourmia.
verdict_text: The Ninja AF101 is the better buy for anyone who cooks regularly—faster, more durable, and more versatile—but the Gourmia GAF400 makes perfect sense if budget and capacity matter most.
seo_content:
I've tested dozens of air fryers, and the Gourmia versus Ninja debate really comes down to what you're willing to spend and how often you'll use it.

The Gourmia GAF400 costs between $60-$80 and gives you a solid 5-quart capacity—plenty of room for a family of four or five. Its 1230W heating element handles standard air frying without issue, and you get eight preset programs that make life easier if you're new to this. The digital controls are simple, everything goes in the dishwasher, and cleanup is painless. The trade-off? Lower wattage means your food takes a bit longer to cook, and the construction feels lighter than pricier models.

Ninja's AF101 sits in the $90-$120 range, and honestly, the difference is noticeable. That 1550W heating element cooks 15-20% faster than the Gourmia, which means crispier results and lower electricity bills over time. I really appreciate the temperature range that drops to 105°F—you can actually dehydrate fruit and make jerky, something the Gourmia simply can't do. The materials feel more durable, the air circulation system works better across different foods, and I expect this unit to last longer. The downside is the 4-quart capacity, which might have you cooking in batches.

For serious home cooks who air fry several times a week, the Ninja pays for itself. The faster cooking and better build quality make it worth the extra cash. But if you're budget-conscious or just testing whether air frying fits your lifestyle, the Gourmia works perfectly fine. That larger basket handles bigger portions, and you'll save $30-40.

Both take up similar counter space, though the Ninja weighs 12.3 lbs versus Gourmia's 10.5 lbs. One thing I noticed: the Ninja's more powerful fan runs louder. If you live in an apartment or have noise sensitivity, that's worth considering.

faq.q: Which air fryer cooks food faster, Gourmia or Ninja?
faq.a: The Ninja AF101 is definitely faster—about 15-20% quicker thanks to its 1550W power versus the Gourmia's 1230W. So chicken wings that need 20 minutes in the Gourmia will be crispy and done in around 16-17 minutes in the Ninja.

faq.q: Can the Gourmia air fryer dehydrate food like the Ninja?
faq.a: No, the Gourmia GAF400 only goes down to 180°F, which is way too hot for dehydrating. The Ninja AF101 drops to 105°F, so you can actually dehydrate fruits, vegetables, and make jerky without any issues.

faq.q: Which air fryer has better capacity for family cooking?
faq.a: The Gourmia GAF400 wins here with its 5-quart basket compared to the Ninja's 4-quart capacity. If you're cooking for 4-5 people regularly, the Gourmia handles it better. The Ninja works great for 2-3 people but you'll be cooking in batches for larger families.

faq.q: Are replacement parts readily available for both brands?
faq.a: Ninja parts are much easier to find—you can grab replacement baskets and accessories at most major retailers. Gourmia parts exist but you'll probably need to order directly from the manufacturer or hunt around specific online stores.

faq.q: Which air fryer is easier to clean?
faq.a: They're pretty much equal on this front. Both have dishwasher-safe, non-stick baskets that wipe down easily. The Ninja's ceramic coating might hold up slightly better against scratches over time, but day-to-day cleaning is the same for both.

faq.q: Is Gourmia GAF400 Digital Air Fryer better than Ninja Air Fryer AF101?
faq.a: No, the Ninja AF101 is the better overall air fryer with superior power (1550W), faster cooking times, and more durable construction. The Gourmia GAF400 only wins on capacity and price, making it better specifically for budget-conscious buyers who need a larger basket.

faq.q: Should I buy Gourmia GAF400 Digital Air Fryer or Ninja Air Fryer AF101?
faq.a: Choose the Ninja AF101 if you cook regularly and want speed, durability, and versatility—the performance difference justifies the higher cost. Choose the Gourmia GAF400 if your budget is tight and you need maximum capacity for feeding a larger family on a smaller budget.

faq.q: What is the difference between Gourmia GAF400 Digital Air Fryer and Ninja Air Fryer AF101?
faq.a: The Ninja AF101 delivers superior performance with 1550W power for faster, more consistent cooking and better build quality for long-term durability. The Gourmia GAF400 compensates with a larger 5-quart basket and significantly lower price, making it ideal for families prioritizing capacity and affordability over cooking speed.
